children.text: The area that includes Naval Base Ventura County, San Nicolas Island, and Begg Rock and the adjacent and surrounding waters within the following coordinates:N. Latitude/W. Longitude33°27.8′/119°34.3′33°20.5′/119°15.5′33°13.5′/119°11.8′33°06.5′/119°15.3′33°02.8′/119°26.8′33°08.8′/119°46.3′33°17.2′/119°56.9′33°30.9′/119°54.2′.

The area that includes Naval Base Coronado, San Clemente Island and the adjacent and surrounding waters running parallel to shore to 3 nautical miles from the high tide line designated by part 165 of title 33, Code of Federal Regulations, on , as the San Clemente Island 3NM Safety Zone.May 20, 2010

children.heading: Activities Within the Southern Sea Otter Military Readiness Areas.—
children.label: b Activities Within the Southern Sea Otter Military Readiness Areas.—

children.heading: .—Incidental takings under endangered species act of 1973
children.label: 1 .—Incidental takings under endangered species act of 1973
children.text: Sections 4 and 9 of the Endangered Species Act of 1973 (, 1538) shall not apply with respect to the incidental taking of any southern sea otter in the Southern Sea Otter Military Readiness Areas in the course of conducting a military readiness activity.16 U.S.C. 1533

children.heading: .—Incidental takings under marine mammal protection act of 1972
children.label: 2 .—Incidental takings under marine mammal protection act of 1972
children.text: Sections 101 and 102 of the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972 (, 1372) shall not apply with respect to the incidental taking of any southern sea otter in the Southern Sea Otter Military Readiness Areas in the course of conducting a military readiness activity.16 U.S.C. 1371

children.heading: .—Treatment as species proposed to be listed
children.label: 3 .—Treatment as species proposed to be listed
children.text: For purposes of conducting a military readiness activity, any southern sea otter while within the Southern Sea Otter Military Readiness Areas shall be treated for the purposes of section 7 of the Endangered Species Act of 1973 () as a member of a species that is proposed to be listed as an endangered species or a threatened species under section 4 of the Endangered Species Act of 1973 ().16 U.S.C. 153616 U.S.C. 1533

children.heading: .—Removal
children.label: c .—Removal
children.text: Nothing in this section or any other Federal law shall be construed to require that any southern sea otter located within the Southern Sea Otter Military Readiness Areas be removed from the Areas.

children.heading: .—Revision or Termination of Exceptions
children.label: d .—Revision or Termination of Exceptions
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior may revise or terminate the application of subsection (b) if the Secretary of the Interior, in consultation with the Secretary of the Navy, determines that military activities occurring in the Southern Sea Otter Military Readiness Areas are impeding the southern sea otter conservation or the return of southern sea otters to optimum sustainable population levels.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary of the Navy shall conduct monitoring and research within the Southern Sea Otter Military Readiness Areas to determine the effects of military readiness activities on the growth or decline of the southern sea otter population and on the near-shore ecosystem. Monitoring and research parameters and methods shall be determined in consultation with the Service.

children.heading: .—Reports
children.label: 2 .—Reports
children.text: Not later than , and every three years thereafter, the Secretary of the Navy shall report to Congress and the public on monitoring undertaken pursuant to paragraph (1).November 25, 2017

children.heading: .—Southern sea otter
children.label: 1 .—Southern sea otter
children.text: The term “southern sea otter” means any member of the subspecies Enhydra lutris nereis.

children.label: A
children.text: when used in reference to activities subject to regulation by the Endangered Species Act of 1973 ( et seq.), shall have the meaning given such term in that Act; and16 U.S.C. 1531

children.label: B
children.text: when used in reference to activities subject to regulation by the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972 ( et seq.) shall have the meaning given such term in that Act.16 U.S.C. 1361

children.heading: .—Incidental taking
children.label: 3 .—Incidental taking
children.text: The term “incidental taking” means any take of a southern sea otter that is incidental to, and not the purpose of, the carrying out of an otherwise lawful activity.

children.heading: .—Military readiness activity
children.label: 4 .—Military readiness activity
children.text: The term “military readiness activity” has the meaning given that term in section 315(f) of the Bob Stump National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2003 ( note) and includes all training and operations of the armed forces that relate to combat and the adequate and realistic testing of military equipment, vehicles, weapons, and sensors for proper operation and suitability for combat use.16 U.S.C. 703

children.heading: .—Optimum sustainable population
children.label: 5 .—Optimum sustainable population
children.text: The term “optimum sustainable population” means, with respect to any population stock, the number of animals that will result in the maximum productivity of the population or the species, keeping in mind the carrying capacity of the habitat and the health of the ecosystem of which they form a constituent element.
